heading: Manner of use of buildings; contracts for construction, etc.
label: 294 Manner of use of buildings; contracts for construction, etc.
text: Buildings and grounds acquired under this chapter or prior to , acquired or authorized for the use of the diplomatic and consular establishments in foreign countries may be used, in the case of buildings and grounds for the diplomatic establishment, as Government offices or residences or as such offices and residences; or, in the case of other buildings and grounds, as such offices or such offices and residences. The contracts for purchases of buildings, for leases, and for all work of construction, alteration, and repair under this chapter are authorized to be negotiated, the terms of the contracts to be prescribed, and the work to be performed, where necessary, without regard to such statutory provisions as relate to the negotiation, making, and performance of contracts and performance of work in the United States and without regard to section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31.May 7, 1926
notes: Codification“Section 3324(a) and (b) of title 31” substituted in text for “section 3648 of the Revised Statutes of the United States ()” on authority of , , , the first section of which enacted Title 31, Money and Finance.31 U.S.C. 529Pub. L. 97–258, § 4(b)96 Stat. 1067Sept. 13, 1982

Amendments1991— inserted “purchases of buildings, for leases, and for” after “contracts for”.Pub. L. 102–1381963— struck out “, subject to the direction of the commission,” before “be used” and “in the judgment of the commission,” after “where necessary,” and inserted “and without regard to ”.Pub. L. 88–94section 529 of title 31
